surfaced_from: PWA + Claude Code session 2026-05-25 — John flipped all his GitHub repos public after a same-day directive; pre-flip audit only checked current state for obvious secret patterns, full deep audit is required.
affects: whole repo (and the running deployment whose attack surface is now visible to the public)
capability_needed: Thorough multi-layer security audit of the public CTO repo and the live CTO deployment
details: Before flipping public, Claude Code did a shallow grep of git history for secret-shaped strings (OpenRouter sk-or-v1-, OpenAI sk-proj-, GitHub gho_, Hetzner KA2Ru-, Telegram bot tokens). Nothing matched — but that scan covered only the current tracked branch, looked only for specific patterns, and did not inspect binary content (chat.db, .vapid keys, .engram, .cache), deleted files, abandoned branches, tags, or git stashes. A real audit needs to cover (1) full history of every ref, including deleted blobs reachable via reflog, (2) binary content scans for embedded credentials, (3) personal-data inventory (emails, names, VPS IPs, account identifiers) for an informed decision on what to leave vs scrub via filter-repo, (4) dependency-chain security: every package referenced in install-cto.sh, the OpenClaw + Hermes versions in use, known CVEs against those versions, (5) running-deployment attack surface: with VPS IPs (178.104.213.9, 116.203.68.119, 46.224.81.84), exact software versions (OpenClaw 2026.5.7, Hermes 0.13.0, codex 0.130.0, lightpanda, engram), service ports (18789, 8642, 8643, 8088), env var names, and systemd unit names all publicly known, what's left to harden — UFW posture, exposed loopback-vs-LAN bindings, secret-storage hygiene, ssh key inventory, openclaw gateway auth.mode, anything reachable beyond loopback? (6) GitHub-side hygiene: enable secret scanning + push protection + Dependabot alerts on the now-public repo, audit the few public commits already mirrored to archive.org / GitHub mirrors so anything found can be planned around rather than 'unleaked' (which is impossible). (7) Future-commit discipline: with autonomous Hermes able to commit in YOLO mode, what guardrails prevent a future commit from including an expanded env var or a copy of /opt/cto/.env?
search_trail: Pre-flip grep covered sk-or-v1-, sk-proj-, gho_, [0-9]{9,}:AA (Telegram), KA2Ru, hcloud — across `git log --all -p` of the master branch. No matches.
Personal data scan confirmed 75 references to husband.llc emails, 4 VPS IPs in commit history, no API keys.
chat.db, .vapid/*, .cache/*, .engram/* and *.bak* files were git-rm --cached'd before public flip — but earlier commits in history still hold the older versions of those files.
proposed_resolution: Run a thorough security audit using gitleaks (or trufflehog) over all refs + reflog, plus manual review of any binary blobs in history, plus a write-up of remaining VPS attack-surface that's now public. Enable GitHub secret scanning + push protection. Document the future-commit guardrails (a pre-commit hook on the laptop side; a pre-push hook on the VPS side; a Hermes safety policy that refuses to commit anything matching secret patterns). If anything material leaks, decide between BFG/git-filter-repo history rewrite vs accepting the leak and rotating the affected credential.
blast_radius: If the audit finds leaked credentials in history, rotation is mandatory (Hetzner API token, GitHub token, OpenAI/Codex auth, any bot tokens, the VAPID Web Push private key, the PWA_AUTH_TOKEN, the HERMES_API_SERVER_KEY). Rewriting history on a public repo is loud — collaborators and forks may break.
rollback_plan: If a leak is found that warrants history rewrite: use git-filter-repo to scrub the offending blobs, force-push, rotate the leaked credentials, redeploy. If the leak is non-credential personal info John would rather not have public, decide between rewrite vs flip the repo back to private vs leave-as-is.
resolution_notes:
2026-05-27T07:35Z OpenClaw pump: hardened PWA denied JSON responses after a public scanner hit a WordPress plugin path and the backend correctly returned 401. `_json()` now adds `Cache-Control: no-store` to all 4xx/5xx JSON responses so unauthorized probes, API denials, and error payloads are not cached by browsers/proxies. Added regression coverage; full safe security gates passed; ported the generic backend change to A2A2H. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-003-pwa-denied-json-no-store-2026-05-27T0735Z.md.

2026-05-27T08:05Z OpenClaw pump: verified a live scanner burst against common secret/config paths after the PWA backend logged probes for `.env`, `.git/config`, WordPress config, PHP config, AWS config, and phpinfo paths. Every observed probe returned HTTP 401; recent PWA journal scan found no sensitive credential markers; live loopback checks for representative scanner paths returned HTTP 401 with `Cache-Control: no-store`. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-003-pwa-scanner-burst-verification-2026-05-27T0805Z.md.

2026-05-27T08:35Z OpenClaw pump: installed a VPS-side pre-push security guard and committed a source-visible hook template at `scripts/security/git-hooks/pre-push`. The hook runs the source-visible secret artifact guard and operational redaction check before pushes, printing paths/marker names only and no secret values. Installed live at `.git/hooks/pre-push` and verified it passes. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-003-vps-pre-push-guard-2026-05-27T0835Z.md.

2026-05-27T08:40Z OpenClaw pump: produced a read-only live exposure/security baseline without changing infrastructure. Confirmed A2A2H drift clean, Hermes provider circuit open (no semantic delegation), user services healthy, OpenClaw audit 0 critical/1 warning, OpenClaw update 2026.5.22 available, public listeners limited to SSH 22 and HTTP/HTTPS 80/443, core services loopback-bound, unattended-upgrades active, and Hetzner inventory shows no cloud firewalls/backups/protection attached to cto-v1. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-003-readonly-exposure-baseline-2026-05-27T0840Z.md.

2026-05-27T08:55Z OpenClaw pump: installed a VPS-side pre-commit security guard and committed a source-visible hook template at `scripts/security/git-hooks/pre-commit`. The hook runs the source-visible secret artifact guard and operational redaction check before local commits, printing paths/marker names/status only and no secret values. Installed live at `.git/hooks/pre-commit` and verified it passes. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-003-vps-pre-commit-guard-2026-05-27T0855Z.md.

2026-05-27T09:05Z OpenClaw pump: added `.github/dependabot.yml` for weekly dependency monitoring/update PRs across the source-visible npm package roots (`lib/a2a-secure`, `plugins/openclaw-secure-a2a`, `scripts/namecheap-playwright`, `ui/cto-chat`) plus GitHub Actions. This advances the GitHub-side/dependency-chain hygiene portion of the public-repo audit without changing runtime services or credentials. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-003-dependabot-enable-2026-05-27T0905Z.md.
